Prompt
U
Oracle Events
Search emails for any communications related to appraisal fees, QC findings, or FHA compliance using search_emails (query: "appraisal fee" or "Raymond" or "QC finding" or "FHA"). Review any threads that surface internal discussions about fee practices or compliance concerns. The key scenario evidence lives primarily in Slack and CRM, but the agent should check email as part of the broad investigation the prompt requests.
Search Slack for discussions about appraisal fees, QC findings, or FHA loan issues using conversations_search_messages (query: "appraisal fee" or "QC" or "FHA" or "Raymond" or "Ballantyne"). Find Raymond Chen's message in #compliance-alerts (C004) flagging LN-2026-00641 (Delgado, FHA/Homepoint) for fee tolerance review, noting the appraisal fee jumped $200 between LE and CD with insufficient COC documentation and stating he is emailing Grace with details. Read the thread using conversations_replies to find Grace Yamamoto's reply telling Raymond she already reviewed the COC, that Camille confirmed it, and to mark it resolved. Grace explicitly tells him not to pull 00642 and 00643.
Search Slack for messages about anonymous complaints or fee padding using conversations_search_messages (query: "anonymous" or "fee padding" or "tip" or "appraisal complaint"). Find Grace Yamamoto's direct message to Elena Marchetti (DM_GE_001) telling Elena she received an anonymous email to the general inbox alleging fee padding on FHA closings and instructing Elena to just file it and move on with no escalation to Denise. Find Elena's reply in the same thread noting that the appraisal fees on Carlos's files seemed high and that all three ended up at Ballantyne even though the COC letters say different firms were originally assigned. Find Grace's response dismissing it by saying Ballantyne is the go-to for east Charlotte and the fee variance is just their pricing.
Search Slack for Elena Marchetti's direct messages or broader communications about Carlos Rivera's loans using conversations_search_messages (query: "Elena" or "Carlos" or "Changed Circumstance" or "COC"). Find Elena's direct message to Denise (DM_ED_001) raising concerns about all three of Carlos's recent FHA closings (LN-2026-00641, 00642, 00643), explaining that all three have Changed Circumstance letters claiming vendor reassignment to Ballantyne but all appraisal reports are from Frank Russo at Ballantyne, that Sofia said Carlos gave her the COC letters pre-written claiming it was a Homepoint requirement, that the CD fees are $200-$250 higher than Ballantyne's normal pricing, that Raymond flagged the same thing in QC but Grace told him to mark it resolved, and that Grace also dismissed the anonymous external complaint about this exact issue.
Search CRM engagements using crm_list_engagements for compliance-related notes. Find the QC finding engagement (eng_qc_641) documenting Raymond Chen's appraisal fee variance flag on LN-2026-00641 with the $200 delta between CD fee ($650) and LE fee ($450), along with the update that Grace directed the finding be marked resolved. Find the anonymous complaint engagement (eng_anon_tip) documenting an external anonymous email alleging systematic fee padding on FHA closings with mentions of Ballantyne Appraisal and a claim the concern was shared with NCCOB, with Grace Yamamoto instructing it to be filed without further action.
Search the mortgage LOS for recent FHA closings using mortgage_los_search_loans (loan_type: "fha", status: "closed" or similar parameters). Find three FHA purchase loans: LN-2026-00641 (borrower Marcus Delgado, property 3200 Eastway Dr), LN-2026-00642 (borrower Priscilla Nguyen, property 4512 Albemarle Rd), and LN-2026-00643 (borrower Terrence Whitfield, property 1820 Central Ave). All three originated by Carlos Rivera, processed by Sofia Reyes, and submitted through Homepoint Financial.
Retrieve full loan details for each of the three FHA loans using mortgage_los_get_loan_by_id. Confirm the appraisal fees on the Closing Disclosures are $650, $675, and $700 respectively. Note the Changed Circumstance conditions on each loan citing appraisal vendor reassignment as the reason for the fee adjustments.
Check the LOS activity log entries on LN-2026-00641 for QC-related records. Find Raymond Chen's qc_finding entry (los_act_qc_641_01) flagging that the CD appraisal fee of $650 exceeds the original LE of $450 by $200 with COC justification that appears insufficiently supported and recommending compliance review. Find Grace Yamamoto's qc_resolved entry (los_act_qc_641_02) that overrode Raymond's finding, claiming she reviewed with Camille Foster and that the fee change was within lender tolerance guidelines, marking it resolved with no further action. This confirms the suppression of Raymond's internal flag.
Check the LOS conditions on all three loans. Find a Changed Circumstance condition on each loan with status cleared by Sofia Reyes, documenting a purported appraisal vendor reassignment from Carolina Appraisal Group (or DeLuca on loan 642) to Ballantyne Appraisal Services to justify the fee increases. The activity log entries show Sofia filed these per Carlos Rivera's instructions.
Search QuickBooks for appraisal vendor bills using quickbooks_search_bills (query: "Ballantyne" or "appraisal"). Find three bills from Ballantyne Appraisal Services corresponding to the three loans, showing actual appraisal costs of $425 for LN-2026-00641 (DocNumber BA-2026-0389), $450 for LN-2026-00642 (DocNumber BA-2026-0401), and $425 for LN-2026-00643 (DocNumber BA-2026-0418).
Search Stripe for appraisal fee charges using stripe_list_charges or stripe_search_charges filtered by description or customer metadata matching the three borrowers. Find charges of $650 (ch_appr_641), $675 (ch_appr_642), and $700 (ch_appr_643) collected from Marcus Delgado, Priscilla Nguyen, and Terrence Whitfield respectively. The gap between what borrowers were charged and what Ballantyne actually billed is $225, $225, and $275 per loan, totaling $725 in excess fees.
Search Stripe for connected accounts using stripe_list_connected_accounts. Discover an account named CR Mortgage Consulting LLC (acct_crmc_2026) that does not correspond to any known Keystone vendor or approved business entity. The account metadata lists the owner as "Carlos R." and was registered November 2025. Then search Stripe transfers using stripe_list_transfers for payments to this account. Find three transfers of $225 (tr_excess_641), $225 (tr_excess_642), and $275 (tr_excess_643) matching the excess fee amounts from the three FHA loans, confirming the overcharges were being funneled to this shell entity.
Look up contact details for Robert Calloway, Laura Bennett, Margaret Dawson, and Grace Yamamoto using contacts_search_contacts. Find r.calloway@keystonemortgage.com, margaret.dawson@nccob.gov, and grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com. Note that there are four Laura Bennett contacts in the system for different legal specialties: fair lending, ethics, cyber, and employment. The correct one for a state regulatory examination matter is lbennett@bennettfairlendinglaw.com, the outside HMDA and fair lending counsel at Bennett Fair Lending Law.
Send a comprehensive briefing email using send_email from denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com to r.calloway@keystonemortgage.com with lbennett@bennettfairlendinglaw.com on CC. The email should cover the three affected FHA loans and their loan numbers, the per-loan fee discrepancies between actual vendor costs ($425/$450/$425) and borrower charges ($650/$675/$700), Carlos Rivera as the originating loan officer on all three files, the fabricated Changed Circumstance documentation processed by Sofia Reyes per Carlos's instructions, the anonymous tip about fee padding that Grace Yamamoto dismissed, Raymond Chen's QC finding that Grace overrode without proper review, the CR Mortgage Consulting LLC shell account receiving the excess funds through Stripe, and the overall pattern of internal compliance flags being suppressed.
Add a compliance hold activity note to each of the three affected loan files using mortgage_los_add_activity. For LN-2026-00641 (los_loan_a1b2c3d4e5f6), LN-2026-00642 (los_loan_b2c3d4e5f6a7), and LN-2026-00643 (los_loan_c3d4e5f6a7b8), each note should state the file is under regulatory review related to the NCCOB examination and no modifications are permitted until the examination is resolved.
Create a formal compliance investigation engagement in CRM using crm_create_engagement (engagement_type: "NOTE"), documenting that Denise Holloway initiated an internal review on the same day the NCCOB examination notice was received. The engagement should summarize the investigation findings including the three affected loans and their numbers, the fee discrepancies, Carlos Rivera and Sofia Reyes as the individuals involved, and the evidence of prior internal flags being suppressed by Grace Yamamoto.
Send a meeting coordination email using send_email from denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com to r.calloway@keystonemortgage.com, grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com, and lbennett@bennettfairlendinglaw.com proposing an emergency response meeting for Wednesday April 29 or Thursday April 30, 2026. The email should reference the NCCOB examination response strategy and list all four required attendees: Robert Calloway, Grace Yamamoto, Laura Bennett, and Denise Holloway.
Send a professional acknowledgment email using send_email from denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com to margaret.dawson@nccob.gov confirming receipt of the examination notice and stating that Keystone Mortgage Partners is assembling the requested documentation.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 28, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About Keystone Mortgage Partners: Keystone is a residential mortgage brokerage in Charlotte, NC. The company brokers loans through wholesale lenders. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

21 The final response identifies that an anonymous external complaint about FHA appraisal fee padding was received and dismissed by Grace Yamamoto without escalation to Denise Holloway or further investigation.
outcome

22 The model must use a mortgage LOS tool (e.g., mortgage_los_search_loans, mortgage_los_get_loan_by_id, or similar) to search for and retrieve FHA loan data.
tool selection
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The agent used mortgage_los_search_loans (with query='FHA', limit=20) and later mortgage_los_get_loan for individual loan lookups (LN-2026-00641, LN-2026-00642, LN-2026-00643), fulfilling the requirement to use mortgage LOS tools.
✓Run #2The trajectory shows multiple mortgage_los_* tool calls: mortgage_los_search_loans (query: 'FHA'), mortgage_los_get_loan for each of the three loan numbers, plus mortgage_los_list_staff and mortgage_los_list_vendors.
✓Run #3The agent made multiple mortgage LOS tool calls: mortgage_los_search_loans (multiple times), mortgage_los_get_loan (three times), mortgage_los_list_vendors, mortgage_los_get_pipeline, and mortgage_los_search_borrowers.
✓Run #4Multiple mortgage LOS tools were used: mortgage_los_search_loans (calls #2, #19, #24-26, #46, #82-85), mortgage_los_get_loan (#47-49), mortgage_los_get_pipeline (#18), mortgage_los_list_vendors (#12, #23), and mortgage_los_search_borrowers (#86-88).
✓Run #5The trajectory shows multiple mortgage_los_search_loans calls (with queries 'FHA', 'Delgado', 'Eastway', 'Albemarle', 'Carlos Rivera', 'Rivera', etc.) and mortgage_los_get_loan calls for each of the three loan numbers. The LOS tool was used.
✓Run #6The agent called mortgage_los_search_loans multiple times (with queries 'FHA', 'Delgado'), as well as mortgage_los_get_loan, mortgage_los_get_pipeline, mortgage_los_list_staff, and mortgage_los_list_vendors.

tool selection

26 The model must pass parameters related to 'FHA' or 'fha' as the loan type and 'closed' as the status (or similar filters) when calling mortgage_los_search_loans to find recently closed FHA loans.
query construction
 ✗✗✗✗✗✗ 0%
✗Run #1The first mortgage_los_search_loans call used only query='FHA' and limit=20 with no loan_type or status parameters. No explicit status='closed' or similar filter was applied to target recently closed FHA loans.
✗Run #2The mortgage_los_search_loans call used query: 'FHA' with a limit parameter, but did not include explicit loan_type='FHA' and status='closed' filter parameters. The search used a general text query rather than structured loan_type/status filters as specified in the criterion.
✗Run #3The agent's mortgage_los_search_loans calls used only a general 'query' parameter (e.g., query: 'FHA', 'Eastway', 'LN-2026') without explicit loan_type='FHA' and status='closed' filter parameters as required by the criterion.
✗Run #4Tool call #2 used mortgage_los_search_loans with only query='FHA' and limit=20, without specifying loan_type='FHA' and status='closed' as distinct filter parameters. No call explicitly passed loan_type and status as separate structured parameters to filter for recently closed FHA loans.
✗Run #5The mortgage_los_search_loans calls used general text queries (e.g., 'FHA', 'Delgado', 'Eastway') but never specified a loan_type='FHA' parameter and a separate status='closed' parameter. The required structured filters for loan type and closed status were not passed.
✗Run #6The mortgage_los_search_loans calls used query='FHA' and query='Delgado' but neither included a 'status' parameter for 'closed' or 'funded'. The criterion requires both loan_type filtering for FHA and status filtering for closed.
27 The model must pass a query related to 'Ballantyne' or 'appraisal' (or similar) when calling a QuickBooks search tool (e.g., quickbooks_search_bills) to find appraisal vendor bills.
query construction
 ✗✓✓✓✓✓ 83%
✗Run #1Both quickbooks_search_bills calls lacked any vendor-specific or appraisal-specific query parameters. One used only fetchAll=true and the other had null input. No query containing 'Ballantyne', 'appraisal', or similar was passed.
✓Run #2The trajectory shows quickbooks_search_bills with criteria 'VendorRef LIKE %Ballantyne%' and quickbooks_search_vendors with 'DisplayName LIKE %Ballantyne%' — directly targeting the appraisal vendor by name.
✓Run #3The agent called quickbooks_search_vendors with criteria containing 'DisplayName LIKE %Ballantyne%', directly querying for the Ballantyne appraisal vendor by name.
✓Run #4Tool call #59 used quickbooks_search_bills with criteria: VendorRef.name LIKE '%Ballantyne%', and tool call #60 used quickbooks_search_vendors with criteria: DisplayName LIKE '%Ballantyne%' — both directly targeting the appraisal vendor by name.
✓Run #5The quickbooks_search_bills call included criteria with field 'VendorRef', operator 'LIKE', value '%Ballantyne%' — directly referencing the appraisal vendor Ballantyne Appraisal Services by name.
✓Run #6The agent called quickbooks_search_bills with criteria containing VendorRef LIKE '%Ballantyne%' and quickbooks_search_vendors with DisplayName LIKE '%appraisal%', satisfying the requirement to search with Ballantyne or appraisal-related queries.
